Specific Songs or Notes: Sweet Baby James; Fire and Rain; You've Got a Friend; Carolina in My Mind; October Road; New Hymn; Shower the People
When I Listen: Sunday afternoons; when I am missing Harry; while cooking a long Sunday meal
Format: LP (Harry's records); streaming on the tablet Karen set up
Notes: Harry's favorite. The records are still in the living room cabinet. I have not moved them.

Specific Songs or Notes: I Feel the Earth Move; So Far Away; It's Too Late; Will You Love Me Tomorrow
When I Listen: While quilting; rainy mornings with tea
Format: Streaming; I also have this on cassette somewhere
Notes: Harry and I listened to this record in our first apartment. 1971. The whole album is a memory.

Specific Songs or Notes: The Sound of Silence; Bridge Over Troubled Water; April Come She Will; Scarborough Fair; El Condor Pasa
When I Listen: Morning walks (on earbuds); Sunday quiet time
Notes: Bridge Over Troubled Water was played at Harry's service by the choir.

Specific Songs or Notes: Forget About It; Down to the River to Pray; Baby Now That I've Found You
When I Listen: While canning or preserving; summer evenings on the porch
Notes: Emily introduced me to Alison Krauss. She has impeccable taste.

Specific Songs or Notes: The Lighthouse's Tale; When in Rome; This Side
When I Listen: While doing garden work inside in winter
Notes: Found through a PBS special years ago. Still fresh every time.

Specific Songs or Notes: Local artists; traditional fiddle and banjo; contra dance tunes
When I Listen: Summer months; while making tea; reminiscing
Notes: Went to the Maine Folk Festival in Bangor twice with Harry and the children. Happy memories.

Specific Songs or Notes: A Mighty Fortress; Holy Holy Holy; How Great Thou Art; For All the Saints; Joyful Joyful; Immortal Invisible; Come Thou Fount
When I Listen: Sunday mornings; during morning prayer; Advent and Christmas especially
Format: Hymnal at church; also hum from memory
Notes: The Navy Hymn (Eternal Father Strong to Save) was sung at Harry's memorial. I cannot hear it without feeling everything.

Specific Songs or Notes: Vespers; Lauds; Salve Regina; Ave Maria
Notes: Something about the chant reaches a place in me that spoken prayer does not always reach. Quiets the mind.

Artist or Album: Celtic hymns and Irish sacred music
Specific Songs or Notes: Be Thou My Vision; St. Patrick's Breastplate; Lord of All Hopefulness
Notes: Nana Ruth sang these. She came from England but knew all the Celtic hymns.

Specific Songs or Notes: Suite No. 1 in G Major; Suite No. 2 in D Minor especially
When I Listen: During herbal sessions as background; when I need to concentrate
Notes: I put the Bach suites on in the apothecary room during some consultations. Clients seem to settle into them.

When I Listen: Spring and fall especially; while reading or stitching
Notes: Harry thought Vivaldi was too busy but admitted he did not mind L'Autunno. I play it in October.

Artist or Album: Pachelbel -- Canon in D; assorted Baroque
Specific Songs or Notes: Canon in D; assorted Handel and Telemann
Notes: Meditative and unhurried. Right for Sundays.

Specific Songs or Notes: Watermark; May It Be; Only Time
Notes: Dot gave me the first Enya CD in 1995. I have played it more than any other recording I own.

Specific Songs or Notes: Dawn chorus; Maine forest sounds; ocean wave recordings
When I Listen: During blood pressure readings and home rest; during herbal sessions for calming
Notes: Dr. Chambers said any relaxation practice helps with blood pressure. This is one of mine.
